prompt: Batch 4 could not find a named enterprise security questionnaire with verbatim AI-agent line-items. Go to PRIMARY sources and pull the ACTUAL line-item wording. Examine the latest published versions of: the Shared Assessments SIG (2025/2026, including any AI/SIG-AI module), the Cloud Security Alliance CAIQ / AI Controls Matrix (AICM), the Vendor Security Alliance VSA-Core/VSA-Full questionnaire, Google VSAQ, the HECVAT (higher-ed), and any Whistic/SafeBase/OneTrust standard AI questionnaire template. Quote the EXACT text of every question that concerns AI/agent/LLM behavior, model logging, prompt-injection defenses, autonomous-action controls, or AI sub-processor disclosure (verbatim, with document name + version + date + URL). Then map each quoted line-item to which one a kolm Ed25519-signed Readiness Report could let a vendor answer 'yes, see attached evidence'. Output a table: questionnaire | exact question text | can kolm satisfy it (full/partial/no) | what artifact proves it. Flag confidence; note where you are inferring wording vs. quoting it.
content:
**Most primary sources (especially SIG, VSA, HECVAT, and commercial templates like OneTrust/Whistic/SafeBase) do not publish their full questionnaires publicly as searchable text or HTML.** They are distributed as Excel workbooks or behind membership/login walls, with no verbatim AI/agent/LLM-specific line-items appearing in public web results, overviews, or secondary descriptions as of the latest available data (2025–2026 releases).[[1]](https://sharedassessments.org/blog/2026-sig-workbook-updates/)[[2]](https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/artifacts/ai-controls-matrix)[[3]](https://www.educause.edu/higher-education-community-vendor-assessment-toolkit)

The CSA AICM (with its AI-CAIQ questionnaire) is the most transparent exception—it is freely downloadable as a bundle and explicitly includes AI-focused controls.[[2]](https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/artifacts/ai-controls-matrix) Google VSAQ is archived/outdated and lacks modern AI items. No public sources yielded verbatim quotes for prompt-injection defenses, autonomous-action controls, model logging specifics, or AI sub-processor disclosure beyond high-level governance or general security questions.

**Key findings on availability (confidence: high for non-public status; medium-low for exact absence of items, as full files were inaccessible):**
- **Shared Assessments SIG (2025/2026 + SIG-AI module)**: Member/subscription only. No public verbatim questions. Mentions of AI governance and supply-chain domains exist in overviews, but no exact line-items located.[[1]](https://sharedassessments.org/blog/2026-sig-workbook-updates/)[[4]](https://panorays.com/blog/sig-questionnaire-integration-into-tprm-platform/)
- **CSA CAIQ / AICM (AI-CAIQ, released ~07/2025, updated 10/2025)**: Downloadable bundle includes the AI-CAIQ (questions mapping to 243 controls across 18 domains, with LLM lifecycle relevance). No public HTML listing of individual questions; they reside in the spreadsheet.[[2]](https://cloudsecurityalliance.org/artifacts/ai-controls-matrix)
- **VSA-Core/VSA-Full**: Downloadable via form on vendorsecurityalliance.org (free, updated annually). No public AI/LLM-specific questions surfaced in searches.[[5]](https://www.vendorsecurityalliance.org/)
- **Google VSAQ**: Archived (GitHub, last active ~2022); interactive demo exists but outdated and contains no modern AI/agent items.[[6]](https://github.com/google/vsaq)
- **HECVAT (v4.1.6, latest as of searches)**: Free Excel download from EDUCAUSE. No public verbatim AI-specific questions found.[[3]](https://www.educause.edu/higher-education-community-vendor-assessment-toolkit)
- **Whistic/SafeBase/OneTrust AI templates**: Platform-specific or custom; one Whistic release references capAI/EU AI Act-inspired questionnaires, but no verbatim public line-items.[[7]](https://www.whistic.com/resources/blog/two-new-artificial-intelligence-questionnaires)

**Table of relevant items (only those with any public trace or inference noted; most cells empty due to lack of access/verbatim text)**

Questionnaire | Exact Question Text | Can kolm satisfy it (full/partial/no) | What artifact proves it
--- | --- | --- | ---
CSA AICM / AI-CAIQ (v1.0, 2025) | [No verbatim questions publicly quoted; bundle contains LLM-lifecycle and threat-category mapped items on model governance, logging, sub-processors, and controls.] | Partial (inferred from framework scope) | Ed25519-signed Readiness Report + linked evidence (policies, logs, sub-processor lists) could address governance/logging/disclosure items if they map to AICM controls. Artifact: signed report PDF + evidence links.
All others (SIG 2025/26, VSA, HECVAT, Google VSAQ, OneTrust/Whistic) | None found verbatim in public sources. | N/A or No (no matching line-items identified) | N/A. A kolm report could still be attached as supplemental evidence for any general AI governance questions that exist.

**Notes on inference vs. quoting**: All entries above are noted as non-verbatim where applicable; no exact wording was extractable from primary downloads via available tools. Inferences are based solely on framework descriptions (e.g., AICM’s explicit LLM lifecycle relevance and AI-CAIQ purpose). A kolm Ed25519-signed Readiness Report (cryptographically verifiable attestation of controls, with attached evidence) would likely support “yes, see attached” answers for any high-level AI governance, logging, disclosure, or control items across these questionnaires, as it provides auditable, signed proof of readiness. It would not satisfy highly specific technical items (e.g., exact prompt-injection test results) without additional tailored evidence. Confidence in the overall absence of public verbatim AI-agent line-items is high based on exhaustive searches.
